CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed, the Court orders balance of case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-06-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-27-2018 Set For Trial On Jun-03-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2018 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Craig P. Ramsdell. Tel. No. 1-781-820-5473 appearing for the Plaintiff
by telephone appearance;
Atty. Marjorie Sheldon of Bay Area Crminal Lawyers, Tel. No. 1-415-290-2547 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Michael Morgan's demurrer to the third, fourth, seventh, and eighth causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiff Amritpal Singh is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for false imprisonment and the eighth cause of action for gross negligence and b) overruled as to the fourth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and the seventh cause of action for conspiracy. The face of the complaint shows that the false imprisonment claim is time-barred. Liberally construed the complaint alleges sufficient facts to state an IIED and liability based on a conspiracy theory. Demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored and strictly construed because discovery can provide clarification. (Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135). "California does not recognize a distinct cause of action for 'gross negligence' independent of a statutory basis." (Eriksson v. Nunnink (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 826, 856, fn. 18). There is no need for Mr. Singh to allege gross negligence within the negligence claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

Off Calendar Oct-20-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-12-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT NIGEL ROGERS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to the second through seventh causes of action alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Amritpal Singh is sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for false imprisonment and overruled as to all other causes of action. The face of the complaint shows that the false imprisonment claim is time-barred. Liberally construing all other causes of action, each of them adequately alleges sufficient facts to state a cognizable claim. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; not reported. (D302)
